findings.claim: Recurrent A-initial positional enrichment in Linear A administrative units (anonymous initial slot; relative constraint). Prefixhood, productivity, sound, and meaning are not established.
findings.claim_source: curated (corrected wording; ledger name/verdict verbatim in the master table)
findings.verdict: REPLICATED_RELATIVE_CONSTRAINT — under the corrected categorical joint null (M=10000), A is the top best-of-universe maxT survivor at p<=.01 under 3/3 overlapping segmentation/selection variants derived from a single corpus lineage (A_editor / B_divider_strict / C_numeral_anchored; B shares 87.6% of unit realizations with A_editor, C is a function-restricted subset of A_editor — robustness across variants, NOT independent replication); PC power+cal clean; LOMO evaluated MECHANICALLY from the frozen E022/E023/E024 artifacts (sha256-recorded; 3/3 independently significant; dropping any one leaves 2). Verdict class UNCHANGED from the original run. Survivor-set diff vs original: A_editor and B_divider_strict unchanged {A,KU,U,I,QA,*411,*306}; C_numeral_anchored gains boundary sign DA -> {A,KU,PA,DA}. No phonetic value, morphological prefixhood, or productivity asserted (L2/L3).
